element.ToolTip: If this value is false, the range is not included in the configuration.
element.Caption: Enabled

element.ToolTip: Defines the version of IP address style to be used for describing the range.
element.Caption: Protocol

element.ToolTip: Defines the base address to be used for enumerating all the addresses in the range.
element.Caption: Address

element.ToolTip: Defines the length (in bits) of the mask to be used in conjunction with all the addresses created in the range. e.g., a prefix of 24 = 255.255.255.0 for IPv4.
element.Caption: Mask

element.ToolTip: Defines the increment to be used for enumerating all the addresses in the range.
element.Caption: Increment

element.ToolTip: The total number of addresses to be created for the range.
element.Caption: Count

element.ToolTip: Defines the gateway to be associated with all the addresses created in the range.
element.Caption: Gateway

element.ToolTip: The Maximum Segment Size, defines the maximum length of the data. TCP MSS = MTU - TCP header size - IP header size. Theoretically, this value can be as large as 65495, but such a large value is never used For Ethernet the maximum value for MSS is 1460 = 1500-40.
element.Caption: MSS

element.ToolTip: The VlanId range from the Ethernet plugin used to associate the Vlan info from. See VlanIdRange for more details.
element.Caption: VLAN Range

element.ToolTip: The MAC range from the Ethernet plugin used to associate the interfaces from. See MacRange for more details.
element.Caption: MAC Range

element.ToolTip: The ATM range from the ATM plugin used to associate the ATM info from. See AtmRange for more details.
element.Caption: Atm Range

element.ToolTip: The PVC range from the ATM plugin used to associate the PVC info from. See PvcRange for more details.
element.Caption: Pvc Range

element.ToolTip: If set, MAC addresses will be auto-generated based on IP
element.Caption: Autogenerate MAC
